channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Carolina sets date for 5th execution in under 7 months
item.description: A South Carolina inmate is set to become the fifth person to be put to death since the state resumed executions in September after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and kills 3 as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday caused deadly crashes, damaged buildings and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states, prompting evacuation orders in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters: A “high risk” day of severe weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After Columbia arrests, international college students fall silent
item.description: International students and foreign faculty have watched the crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters at Columbia University with apprehension.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar thinks Selena 'partly to blame' for death: Report
item.description: Saldívar, 64, officially filed for parole in January. She shot Selena during a confrontation in a Texas hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he was being a 'bit sarcastic' when he promised to end Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday that he was “being a little bit sarcastic” when he repeatedly claimed as a candidate that he would have the Russia-Ukraine war solved within 24 hours — and even before he even took office.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: 911 audio released in Idaho student murders
item.description: Authorities have unveiled the recording of a panicked 911 call placed after the stabbings of four University of Idaho students in November 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: FAA 'permanently restricting' Washington helicopter traffic after fatal midair collision near DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration announced a "series of steps" it will take to address safety concerns after a fatal midair crash near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa's Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: A collaboration decades in the making, Grammy-award winning rapper Doechii joined "Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" alums Will Smith and Tatyana Ali to recreate a viral TikTok trend.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission votes to pursue prosecution against State Supt. Walters
item.description: On Thursday, the Oklahoma Ethics Commission voted to pursue prosecution against State Superintendent Ryan Walters for alleged campaign violations.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Brown / KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What's Pi Day all about? Math, science, pies and more
item.description: Math enthusiasts around the world, from college students to rocket scientists, celebrate Pi Day, which is March 14 or 3/14 — the first three digits of an infinite number with many practical uses.
item.dc:creator: Curt Anderson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent fleeing passengers to a wing amid billowing smoke.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert, Sarah Brumfield, Jesse Bedayn And Coleeen Slevin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: The Trump administration has delivered an extraordinary ultimatum to Columbia University, threatening to permanently end federal funding to the school unless it cedes control of an international studies department and implements sweeping changes to other policies.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Third MLB hat pulled over accidental vulgarity as others sell for hundreds online
item.description: Yet another newly released MLB hat has been pulled from online stores after fans noted the potentially crass design the cap sported.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Arrest made after Austin, Texas 17-vehicle crash leaves 3 adults, 2 kids dead
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas arrested a suspect Friday following a 17-vehicle crash on I-35 that left five people dead, including a child and an infant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: President Donald Trump used a visit to the Justice Department on Friday to air a litany of grievances about the criminal investigations that threatened to torpedo his political career.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to partly allow birthright citizenship restrictions
item.description: The Trump administration is asking the Supreme Court to allow restrictions on birthright citizenship to partly take effect while legal fights play out.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SpaceX Dragon launch to relieve stranded astronauts is delayed again
item.description: Less than an hour before liftoff, the SpaceX Dragon mission to provide assistance to two astronauts was aborted yet again, this time for a ground equipment issue.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA ends program that helped schools serve food from local farmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Agriculture Department is ending two pandemic-era programs that provided more than $1 billion for schools and food banks to purchase food from local farmers and producers.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Risks of state abortion reporting mandates outweigh the benefits, an advocacy group says
item.description: States should stop requiring health providers to file reports on every abortion because the information poses a risk to both them and their patients in the current political environment, a research group that advocates for abortion access says.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump is dismantling the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job.” Since Trump took office, staffing at the Education Department has been cut in half, and he has been mulling an executive order to close the agency.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9
